description: Breaking Fantasy Football player news along with the impact for fantasy managers

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.desc: Jordan Whitehead has been cleared to resume his NFL career after undergoing neck surgery.
items.impact: Whitehead suffered a serious neck injury in a car crash last year that led to him requiring surgery. He has visited the Colts and Texans with more visits expected to be coming up.
items.title: Jordan Whitehead (neck) cleared to resume NFL career

items.author: Garrett Ball
items.desc: The Patriots have signed defensive end Isaiah Iton.
items.impact: Iton didn't play in 2025 after being placed on Injured Reserve, but is getting a second chance in 2026. It's unlikely he is much more than a practice squad addition this season.
items.title: Isaiah Iton signs with New England Patriots

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.desc: The Seahawks placed Tyrone Broden on the PUP list.
items.impact: Broden is still recovering from an injury he suffered last season.
items.title: Tyrone Broden placed on PUP list

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.desc: The Seahawks placed RB Kenny McIntosh on the PUP list.
items.impact: McIntosh is continuing to work his way back from a knee injury he suffered last season.
items.title: Kenny McIntosh placed on PUP list

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.desc: The Seahawks placed Nick Emmanwori on the PUP list.
items.impact: It is unclear what Emmanwori is dealing with, but he won't be ready for the start of training camp. He made an immediate huge impact as a rookie last season for the Super Bowl champions. There should be an update on his status when available.
items.title: Nick Emmanwori placed on PUP list

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.desc: The Packers are hopeful to land a contract extension with Tucker Kraft.
items.impact: Kraft is entering the final year of his deal. He is working his way back from a knee injury and is on track to be ready for Week 1.
items.title: Tucker Kraft hopeful to sign extension with Packers

items.author: Diego Sandoval
items.desc: Devonte Wyatt has agreed to a 3-year, $57 million contract extension with the Green Bay Packers.
items.impact: Wyatt's extension also includes a $20-million signing bonus. The defensive tackle played in 10 games last season and totaled four sacks, six tackles for loss and seven quarterback hits.
items.title: Devonte Wyatt agrees to 3-year extension with Packers

items.author: Garrett Ball
items.desc: Javonte Williams remains as Dallas' lead back by a comfortable margin headed into 2026 following a breakout campaign that ended with him signing a three year extension with the team.
items.impact: Williams has the bell-cow, or at least the lead role, on lockdown. However, what's primarily in question is who will be backing him up, as Jaydon Blue, Malik Davis, and Phil Mafah are all vying for the role. It appears Dallas, while comfortable with Williams, doesn't want to put the same sort of workload on him again this season as he had in 2025. That could hurt his upside unless he becomes more proficient in the passing game, but on volume alone he should maintain RB2 status.
items.title: Javonte Williams remains Cowboys lead back in 2026

items.author: Garrett Ball
items.desc: Can Skattebo said he is "ready to go" during an interview at Fantatics Fest this last weekend. His QB Jaxson Dart, who was also present, added that "he looks good."
items.impact: Skattebo is working his way back from a gruesome ankle injury, but has now gone viral for a trying to show off his healthy status during a somewhat failed backflip attempt at Fanatics Fest. If it's true that he's good to go, Skattebo could easily find himself as an RB1 on the cheap for drafters given the kind of workload he sustained last year and the current offensive system he finds himself in.
items.title: Cam Skattebo 'ready to go' for training camp

items.author: Garrett Ball
items.desc: Darnell Mooney is expected to be the Giants lead slot receiver in 2026, filling the void left by Wan'Dale Robinson.
items.impact: Mooney had a down year in Atlanta in 2025, but has shown the ability to be a capable target in the past and could be a nice value in drafts. Wan'Dale Robinson was peppered with targets each of the  last two seasons (both with and without Malik Nabers), so this could lead to some nice stable PPR production for the veteran.
items.title: Darnell Mooney expected to be Giants lead slot receiver

items.author: Garrett Ball
items.desc: Makai Lemon said he is "feeling great" and will be "100 percent" ahead of Philadelphia's first practice after missing mandatory minicamp.
items.impact: Lemon wasn't cleared to join minicamp due to a hamstring injury, but that appears to be behind him now. The first round rookie appears poised to be the secondary target of the Eagles offense behind Devonta Smith this season, and could be a nice sleeper pick in drafts.
items.title: Makai Lemon 'feeling great' ahead of Eagles first practice

items.author: Diego Sandoval
items.desc: The San Francisco 49ers have placed Khalil Dinkins on the active/non-football injured list.
items.impact: Dinkins signed with the 49ers as an undrafted free agent this offseason, and he has now been placed on the injured list. He had dealt with an injury during his time at Penn State, but the nature and extent of this injury remain unknown.
items.title: Khalil Dinkins placed on active/non-football injured list

items.author: Diego Sandoval
items.desc: Jack Bouwmeester has signed a 3-year contract with the San Francisco 49ers.
items.impact: Bouwmeester is the second punter the 49ers have signed this offseason, as he joins a depth chart that also features Corliss Waitman. In a corresponding move, Khalil Dinkins was placed on the active/non-football injured list.
items.title: Jack Bouwmeester signs 3-year deal with 49ers

items.author: Diego Sandoval
items.desc: Deven Eastern has been placed on the physically unable to perform list with an unspecified injury.
items.impact: The injury is unknown, so Eastern's timetable for return is currently up in the air. Eastern was taken by the Seattle Seahawks in the seventh round of the 2026 NFL Draft.
items.title: Deven Eastern placed on PUP list

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.desc: Ethan Pocic is signing a one-year deal with the Baltimore Ravens.
items.impact: Pocic started 97 games in nine NFL seasons before tearing his Achilles last year. He is expected to be healthy and figures to replace former Ravens center Tyler Linderbaum.
items.title: Ethan Pocic signs one-year deal with Ravens

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.desc: Levi Wallace retired from the NFL after seven seasons.
items.impact: Wallace last played for the Broncos in 2024. After being an undrafted free agent in 2018, he appeared in 96 games during his career with 72 starts.
items.title: Levi Wallace retires from NFL

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.desc: The Panthers re-signed OG Brady Christensen.
items.impact: Christensen made four starts across eight appearances last season. He will remain in a depth role for Carolina.
items.title: Brady Christensen re-signs with Panthers

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.desc: Ian Rapoport reportedly believes that Chiefs QB Patrick Mahomes is "on track to be on the field Week 1."
items.impact: Per Rapoport, "Mahomes has been spending seven hours or so in the building rehabbing as much as humanly possible." If he does make it back in that time, it will be roughly a nine month recovery for him.
items.title: Patrick Mahomes II (knee) on track to be ready for Week 1

items.author: Diego Sandoval
items.desc: George Pickens will officially play the 2026 season on a $27.3 million franchise tag for the Dallas Cowboys.
items.impact: Pickens and the Cowboys could not agree upon a long-term extension this summer, so he will head into the year effectively on a 1-year deal. The wideout is coming off a career year in which he hauled in 93 catches for 1,429 yards and nine touchdowns. He will head into this season with Dallas firmly in the WR1 conversation in fantasy.
items.title: George Pickens to play 2026 season on franchise tag with Cowboys

items.author: Garrett Ball
items.desc: Alvin Kamara will be playing for the Saints in 2026 after he and the team reworked his deal.
items.impact: Kamara will be playing behind newcomer Travis Etienne this season. However, he should find himself with a role as a pass catcher, that has always been specialty. Don't be surprised if he puts up sneaky RB3 numbers in 2026.
items.title: Alvin Kamara staying with New Orleans Saints

items.author: Brenton Kemp
items.desc: Carolina Panthers quarterback Will Grier is retiring.
items.impact: Grier began his career with the Panthers, making his lone two NFL career starts in the 2019 season. He also spent time as a backup with the Cowboys, Chargers, and Eagles before re-signing with the Panthers. The 31-year-old was drafted in the third round by his hometown Panthers in the 2019 NFL Draft.
items.title: Will Grier retires

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.desc: There have reportedly been at least five teams that have been checking in on Stefon Diggs nearly two weeks out from training camp.
items.impact: Diggs is coming off an 1,000 yard season in New England after tearing his ACL. Per Fowler, Diggs believes he can be even better due to offseason training with no injury concern.
items.title: Stefon Diggs drawing interest from at least five teams

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.desc: Baker Mayfield revealed he played with a sprained MCL and PCL after Week 2 of last season.
items.impact: Mayfield revealed the injury while speaking on the Netflix series Quarterback, per Bucs beat reporter Rick Stroud. He added that Mayfield also had a "bad biceps contusion" that affected his velocity, in addition to the left shoulder injury he suffered in Week 12 against the Rams. He should be back to full health heading into this upcoming season.
items.title: Baker Mayfield played with sprained MCL and PCL after Week 2 last season

items.author: Diego Sandoval
items.desc: Isaiah McDuffie has signed a one-year extension with the Green Bay Packers.
items.impact: McDuffie's extension keeps him in Green Bay for the next two seasons, paying him $8.8 million along the way. The linebacker is coming off a season in which he totaled 92 combined tackles in 17 games played.
items.title: Isaiah McDuffie signs extension with Green Bay Packers

items.author: Ari Koslow
items.desc: The Bears signed TE Sam Roush to a four-year, $7.35 million contract.
items.impact: Roush was selected No. 69 overall by Chicago in this year's draft. He will figure to compete for a depth role in the Bears tight end room.
items.title: Sam Roush signs rookie contract with Bears

title: Fantasy Player News
